feature.name: Squire of Solamnia
feature.summary: You gain the Squire of Solamnia feat.
feature.rulesText:
Squire of Solamnia
You gain the Squire of Solamnia feat.
In addition, the Knights of Solamnia provide you free, modest lodging and food at any of their fortresses or encampments.

structuredSections.title: Equipment
structuredSections.text: An insignia of rank, a deck of cards, a set of common clothes, and a pouch containing 10 gp.

structuredSections.title: Squire of Solamnia
structuredSections.text:
You gain the Squire of Solamnia feat.
In addition, the Knights of Solamnia provide you free, modest lodging and food at any of their fortresses or encampments.

structuredSections.title: Building a Knight of Solamnia Character
structuredSections.text:
Any class that has martial prowess can be a good fit in the Knights of Solamnia. Fighters and Paladins make up the bulk of the knighthood’s forces. Clerics (often with the War Domain) can also be found among the knights' ranks.
For a more unusual take on a Knight of Solamnia character, consider playing a Bard of the College of Valor (or the College of Swords) or a Barbarian devoted to the ideals of the nature god Habbakuk (perhaps adopting the Path of the Zealot).
Knight of Solamnia Trinkets. When you make your character, you may roll once on the Knight of Solamnia Trinkets table, instead of on the Trinkets table in the Player's Handbook, for your starting trinket.

structuredSections.title: Knight of Solamnia Trinkets
structuredSections.text:
A flat silver disk you record your heroics upon
A piece of a fallen knight's armor
A pendant featuring a crown, a rose, or a sword
The pommel of your mentor's sword
A meaningful favor from someone you defended—perhaps a handkerchief or glove
A locket with a sketch of a silver dragon inside

documentBlocks.text: You have trained to be a valorous warrior known as a Knight of Solamnia. Strict rules guide your every action, and you work to uphold them as you strive to defend the weak and oppose evil. Your honor is as important to you as your life.
Source: Dragonlance: Shadow of the Dragon Queen

Skill Proficiencies: Athletics, Survival
Languages: Two of your choice
Equipment: An insignia of rank, a deck of cardsValue: 5sp Weight: 0, a set of common clothesValue: 5sp Weight: 3lbs, and a pouchValue: 5sp Weight: 1lb containing 10 gp.
You gain the Squire of Solamnia feat.
In addition, the Knights of Solamnia provide you free, modest lodging and food at any of their fortresses or encampments.
Building a Knight of Solamnia Character

Any class that has martial prowess can be a good fit in the Knights of Solamnia. Fighters and Paladins make up the bulk of the knighthood’s forces. Clerics (often with the War Domain) can also be found among the knights' ranks.

For a more unusual take on a Knight of Solamnia character, consider playing a Bard of the College of Valor (or the College of Swords) or a Barbarian devoted to the ideals of the nature god Habbakuk (perhaps adopting the Path of the Zealot).

Knight of Solamnia Trinkets. When you make your character, you may roll once on the Knight of Solamnia Trinkets table, instead of on the Trinkets table in the Player's Handbook, for your starting trinket.

rows: A flat silver disk you record your heroics upon
A piece of a fallen knight's armor
A pendant featuring a crown, a rose, or a sword
The pommel of your mentor's sword
A meaningful favor from someone you defended—perhaps a handkerchief or glove
A locket with a sketch of a silver dragon inside
